contents.title: IELTS SPEAKING TEST 2017 - DESCRIBE YOUR NEIGHBORS

vocabulary.definition: to communicate or work together with someone or something
examples: Children learn how to interact with others at school.
The software allows users to interact with the data in various ways.
vocabulary.context_sentence: it's all about how we interact with people who are our neighbors

vocabulary.definition: the act of competing; a contest or rivalry
examples: There is fierce competition between the two companies.
The competition for the job was intense.
vocabulary.context_sentence: at first we weren't really good friends because there's some competition between America my country and England

vocabulary.definition: cheerful and without worry
examples: He made a light-hearted joke about the situation.
The atmosphere at the party was light-hearted and relaxed.
vocabulary.context_sentence: but it's light-hearted and fun as I got to know this guy

vocabulary.definition: work that is done to help people who are suffering, especially in a war or natural disaster
examples: The organization provides humanitarian work in disaster-stricken areas.
She dedicated her life to humanitarian work.
vocabulary.context_sentence: and he did a lot of humanitarian work as well

vocabulary.definition: showing politeness and consideration for others
examples: The students were respectful of their teacher.
It's important to be respectful of other cultures.
vocabulary.context_sentence: I'm a good neighbor because I try hard to be respectful

vocabulary.definition: taking care to do things carefully and correctly
examples: She is a conscientious worker and always completes her tasks on time.
The company is conscientious about its environmental impact.
vocabulary.context_sentence: and by being conscientious

vocabulary.definition: practical contact with and observation of facts or events
examples: He has a lot of experience in the field of marketing.
She gained valuable experience working abroad.
vocabulary.context_sentence: maybe there's an older person who has had a lot of experience in some type of business

vocabulary.definition: a feature or quality belonging typically to a person, place, or thing and serving to identify it
examples: One of the main characteristics of this breed is its intelligence.
The city is known for its unique characteristics.
vocabulary.context_sentence: and then describe the characteristics of what a good neighbor is and what a bad neighbor is

vocabulary.definition: showing politeness and consideration for others
examples: The students were respectful of their teacher.
It's important to be respectful of other cultures.
vocabulary.context_sentence: try to be respectful in terms of noise and so on

vocabulary.definition: a person who examines someone or something
examples: The examiner will assess your writing skills.
The examiner asked me some questions about my experience.
vocabulary.context_sentence: to make it more interesting for the IELTS examiner's and also to prevent

vocabulary.definition: to learn something so that you remember it exactly
examples: She spent hours memorizing the poem.
It's not a good idea to memorize answers for the IELTS.
vocabulary.context_sentence: prevent people from preparing too much for the IELTS by memorizing an answer

vocabulary.definition: arranged or planned in a systematic way
examples: The meeting was well-organized.
She is an organized person and always keeps things tidy.
vocabulary.context_sentence: you'll have a very organized way of delivering a great answer

grammar.title: Present Simple for Habits and Facts
grammar.explanation: Used to describe habits, general truths, and repeated actions.
examples: I speak English.
He likes to read.
grammar.context_sentence: He speaks the same language as me but in a very different way

grammar.title: Past Simple Tense
grammar.explanation: Used to describe completed actions in the past.
examples: I went to the store yesterday.
She studied hard for the exam.
grammar.context_sentence: I gave him some books that I thought would be interesting to him

grammar.title: Relative Clauses
grammar.explanation: Used to provide additional information about a noun.
examples: The man who lives next door is a doctor.
The book that I read was interesting.
grammar.context_sentence: I have a neighbor who's from England

grammar.title: Modal Verb 'could' for Possibility
grammar.explanation: Used to express possibility in the past.
examples: He could have gone to the party.
She could have studied harder.
grammar.context_sentence: here's an example of how you could answer those three questions

grammar.title: Conditional Sentences (Type 1)
grammar.explanation: Used to talk about possible situations and their consequences.
grammar.structure: If + present simple, will + base verb
examples: If I study hard, I will pass the exam.
If it rains, I will stay home.
grammar.context_sentence: so if I go to town and buy some special snacks I bring them over for he and his wife

grammar.title: Present Perfect Tense
grammar.explanation: Used to describe actions that started in the past and continue to the present or have a result in the present.
examples: I have lived here for five years.
She has finished her work.
grammar.context_sentence: maybe there's an older person who has had a lot of experience in some type of business

grammar.title: Use of 'and' for listing and connecting ideas
grammar.explanation: Used to connect words, phrases, or clauses of equal importance.
examples: I like apples and oranges.
She is smart and hardworking.

contents.title: IELTS Speaking Part 2: Describe a Childhood Neighborhood

vocabulary.definition: A comfortable and pleasant area to live in.
examples: We lived in a cozy neighborhood with friendly neighbors.
The cafe had a cozy neighborhood feel.
vocabulary.context_sentence: When I was a child, I lived in a cozy neighborhood called Coin Pitch, which was situated on the outskirts of our town.

vocabulary.definition: Located on the outskirts of.
examples: The hotel is situated on a beautiful beach.
The school is situated on the edge of the city.
vocabulary.context_sentence: When I was a child, I lived in a cozy neighborhood called Coin Pitch, which was situated on the outskirts of our town.

vocabulary.definition: A tightly connected and supportive group of people.
examples: The town had a close-knit community where everyone knew each other.
She valued the close-knit community of her neighborhood.
vocabulary.context_sentence: It was a peaceful and close-knit community nestled amid lush greenery and rolling hills.

vocabulary.definition: Abundant and vibrant green plants.
examples: The park was filled with lush greenery.
The house was surrounded by lush greenery.
vocabulary.context_sentence: It was a peaceful and close-knit community nestled amid lush greenery and rolling hills.

vocabulary.definition: Hills that have gentle sloping shapes.
examples: The countryside was dotted with rolling hills.
They enjoyed hiking in the rolling hills.
vocabulary.context_sentence: It was a peaceful and close-knit community nestled amid lush greenery and rolling hills.

vocabulary.definition: Making the area feel welcoming and pleasant.
vocabulary.translation: bầu không khí ấm áp và thân thiện
examples: The restaurant had a warm and friendly atmosphere.
The staff created a warm and friendly atmosphere for the guests.
vocabulary.context_sentence: The neighborhood primarily consisted of families and retirees, creating a warm and friendly atmosphere.

vocabulary.definition: A very enjoyable and positive time.
examples: The trip was a delightful experience.
She had a delightful experience at the concert.
vocabulary.context_sentence: Living in Kangpi was truly a delightful experience.

vocabulary.definition: Houses that are kept in good condition.
examples: The neighborhood had many well-maintained houses.
They take pride in their well-maintained houses.
vocabulary.context_sentence: The streets were lined with beautiful, well-maintained houses, each with its own unique charm.

vocabulary.definition: Feeling of belonging and togetherness.
examples: The school fostered a strong sense of community.
They worked to build a sense of community in their new town.
vocabulary.context_sentence: The neighborhood had a strong sense of community, and neighbors often engaged in activities together, such as organizing street parties, picnics in the park, and holiday celebrations.

vocabulary.definition: Areas outside like parks and gardens.
examples: The city has many outdoor spaces for recreation.
They enjoyed spending time in the outdoor spaces of their home.
vocabulary.context_sentence: One of the standout features of this neighborhood was its emphasis on outdoor spaces.

vocabulary.definition: Paths for walking or hiking.
examples: The park has several walking trails.
They enjoyed hiking on the walking trails.
vocabulary.context_sentence: Additionally, there were several walking trails that wound through the scenic landscape, providing a perfect setting for outdoor enthusiasts and nature lovers.

vocabulary.definition: Beautiful and picturesque scenery.
examples: The area had a scenic landscape.
They enjoyed the scenic landscape during their drive.
vocabulary.context_sentence: Additionally, there were several walking trails that wound through the scenic landscape, providing a perfect setting for outdoor enthusiasts and nature lovers.

vocabulary.definition: A place with people from various cultures.
vocabulary.translation: khu dân cư đa văn hóa
examples: The city is known for its multicultural neighborhoods.
They enjoyed living in a multicultural neighborhood.
vocabulary.context_sentence: The residents of Kangpich were diverse yet unified. It was a multicultural neighborhood where people from different backgrounds and cultures coexisted harmoniously.

vocabulary.definition: Variety of different cultures.
examples: The city celebrates its cultural diversity.
They appreciated the cultural diversity of the community.
vocabulary.context_sentence: This cultural diversity allowed for a rich exchange of ideas and traditions, fostering an environment of acceptance and understanding.

vocabulary.definition: Opportunities for professional advancement.
examples: The job offers excellent career prospects.
They were looking for a city with good career prospects.
vocabulary.context_sentence: my preferences and priorities have shifted, and I now gravitate toward more vibrant urban areas with better career prospects and a wider range of cultural activities.

grammar.title: Past Simple and Past Continuous
grammar.explanation: Used to describe past events and actions in progress.
grammar.structure: Subject + was/were + V-ing (Past Continuous); Subject + V-ed (Past Simple)
examples: When I was a child, I lived in a cozy neighborhood.
The streets were lined with beautiful houses.
grammar.context_sentence: When I was a child, I lived in a cozy neighborhood called Coin Pitch, which was situated on the outskirts of our town.

grammar.title: Relative Clauses
grammar.explanation: Used to provide additional information about a noun.
examples: The neighborhood, which was situated on the outskirts, was peaceful.
The parks that children could freely play in were numerous.
grammar.context_sentence: When I was a child, I lived in a cozy neighborhood called Coin Pitch, which was situated on the outskirts of our town.

grammar.title: Use of 'Although' and 'However'
grammar.explanation: To contrast ideas and show concession.
grammar.structure: Although/Even though + clause, clause; Clause. However, clause.
examples: Although Kangpich offered tranquility, it lacked certain amenities.
I hold dear the memories of my childhood neighborhood. However, I wouldn't necessarily choose to live there in the future.
grammar.context_sentence: Although Kangpich offered tranquility and a close community, it lacked certain amenities and opportunities that I seek in my adult life.

grammar.title: Present Perfect Tense
grammar.explanation: To describe experiences that started in the past and continue to the present or have relevance to the present.
examples: I have cherished memories of growing up in Kangpich.
My preferences and priorities have shifted.
grammar.context_sentence: Reflecting my experiences, I have cherished memories of growing up in Kangpich.

grammar.title: Use of 'would' for hypothetical situations
grammar.explanation: To express hypothetical situations or preferences.
examples: I wouldn't necessarily choose to live there in the future.
I would like to live in a city.
grammar.context_sentence: However, as much as I hold dear the memories of my childhood neighborhood, I wouldn't necessarily choose to live there in the future.

grammar.title: Adverbial Clauses of Reason
grammar.explanation: To explain the reason for something.
examples: As I have grown, my preferences have shifted.
Because it lacked amenities, I wouldn't live there.
grammar.context_sentence: As I have grown, my preferences and priorities have shifted, and I now gravitate toward more vibrant urban areas with better career prospects and a wider range of cultural activities.

grammar.title: Use of 'so' to summarize
grammar.explanation: To summarize or conclude a point.
examples: So, I wouldn't live there.
So, I prefer urban areas.
grammar.context_sentence: So now let's get to the phrases and collocations and their simplified definitions:
